narration: Here's a wild one: MIT researchers proved that paying with a card makes you spend up to 47% more than cash. Your brain literally can't feel the money leaving.
visual: Split screen: left shows a hand holding cash ($100 bill) with a red brain glow and pain signal. Right shows a card tap with brain grayed out, no pain signal. Amber accent on card side.

narration: There's a region of your brain called the insula — it fires when you feel physical pain. Cash triggers it every time. Cards? They basically turn it off.
visual: SVG brain outline center-screen. Cash symbol on left side lights up a glowing hot-spot on the brain. Card symbol on right side — brain stays dark and cold. Labels: CASH = PAIN ACTIVE, CARD = PAIN OFF.

narration: In one study, people bidding with credit cards paid $28 for something cash users paid only $9. That same gap is happening at every coffee shop, restaurant, and checkout line.
visual: Auction podium with two people. Cash bidder holds a $9 sign in green. Card bidder holds a $28 sign in red. Dollar gap counter shows +$19 EXTRA in large amber text.

narration: The average American now carries $6,200 in credit card debt. It's not because they bought one big thing — it's a thousand tiny purchases their brain never registered as painful.
visual: Credit card SVG in center. Around it: coffee cup, phone, shopping bag, restaurant fork — each icon has a small dollar amount ($4, $12, $35, $60). Totals accumulate at bottom to $6,200.

narration: Number one fix: use cash for your discretionary spending — eating out, coffee, clothes, entertainment. Studies show people spend up to $3,000 less per year just from this switch.
visual: Wallet with cash bills fanning out. Icons for dining, coffee, clothes, fun each have a decreasing spend bar. Annual savings counter ticks up to $3,000 in green.

narration: Your card company loves that your brain doesn't feel the pain. Pull out $200 cash on Sunday, and when it's gone it's gone. Start building real wealth instead of debt.
visual: Hand pulling $200 cash from ATM. Piggy bank grows as the week progresses (Mon–Sun day counter). Bold CTA banner: THE $200 SUNDAY RULE. Light background.
